name: UF
noVarsityNote: UF does NOT field a varsity men's soccer team — the SEC only sponsors women's soccer at most member schools, and UF men's soccer is a student-run club, not an NCAA program. Olivier cannot be recruited or scholarshipped here. Listed for academic reference only — UF's APK degree remains the closest US structural match to ACU BESS (16/16).

notable: N/A — no varsity program, no NCAA pathway
Academic-only consideration for graduate school prep
proPlayers.draftRank: N/A — UF does not have a varsity men's soccer program

degreeTitle: B.S. in Applied Physiology and Kinesiology (APK)
acuAlignNote: FULL alignment — UF APK is the closest US structural match to ACU BESS. Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription, resistance training, motor control, sport psych, research, AND mandatory 12cr internship. STEM-designated degree.

gpa.note: UF is selective (APK is a competitive major). GPA 2.8 is borderline — needs strong athletic profile.

fin.internationalNote: UF Bright Futures is state-funded (Florida residents only). International athletes rely on athletic + merit package. APK degree competitive — athletic aid is essential lever.

culture.vibe: Classic large American college-town atmosphere. Gainesville during football season is electric — The Swamp is one of the loudest stadiums in college sports. Heavy Gators sports culture dominates social life. University Avenue bar scene is legendary among college towns.
culture.thingsToDo: Ben Hill Griffin Stadium (Florida Gators football), Payne's Prairie hiking, Ichetucknee Springs tubing, Cedar Key day trips, Gainesville music scene (Tom Petty's hometown), Lake Alice wildlife. Gainesville itself is a small city.
culture.socialScene: Tight-knit college town feel. International student community present but smaller than urban schools. Football Saturdays are unmissable cultural events. Academic culture is serious — library culture strong.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Gainesville is a great college town but not a major city. Less Sydney-like than Florida coastal options. Culture is warm and friendly. Best if immersive college-town experience appeals.
culture.lifestyleTags: College-town, Gators culture, outdoors

facilityDetails.stadium: Donald R. Dizney Stadium — 2,500 capacity, FieldTurf, permanent lighting, press box. Part of the UF Athletic Association complex. SEC production values.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 3 practice fields within the soccer complex. Additional access to UF Athletic Association turf fields. Year-round Gainesville climate allows outdoor training 10+ months annually.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: James G. Pressly Stadium includes dedicated soccer strength & conditioning suite. Access to UF Athletic Association main weight facility (one of the largest in collegiate athletics — 28,000 sq ft). Olympic lifting, squat platforms, pneumatic resistance, cryotherapy, hyperbaric chamber access.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Applied Physiology & Kinesiology (APK) Center for Exercise Science — 13 dedicated laboratories. Includes: metabolic lab (8 metabolic carts), body composition lab (DEXA, BodPod), biomechanics lab (3D motion capture, 8 force plates), neuromuscular lab, environmental physiology chamber (heat/altitude simulation), cardiovascular lab, muscle biopsy suite. The most comprehensive academic exercise science facility in any school in this guide.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UF Health Sports Medicine — dedicated team physician (Dr Christopher Logue, team physician for Jacksonville Jaguars). Sports medicine suite within soccer complex, full rehabilitation gym, underwater treadmill, hydrotherapy. UF Health Shands Hospital (top-10 nationally) available for complex cases.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: APK degree has dedicated lab access built into the curriculum — students aren't just observing, they're running experiments. 12-credit internship at UF Health facilities. Pre-DPT advising program with formal letter-of-recommendation support from APK faculty. Access to UF College of Medicine anatomy labs.
facilityDetails.extras: Ben Hill Griffin Stadium (The Swamp — 90,000 capacity) — seeing Florida Gators football is a college bucket-list experience. UF student ID gives free access to all UF athletic events. O'Dome fitness complex. Student Health Center pharmacy and clinic on campus. UF Norman Gyms for open recreation.
facilityDetails.note: UF's APK facility is unmatched academically in this guide — 13 labs, a hyperbaric chamber, muscle biopsy suite, and embedded curriculum access. If Olivier's primary goal is DPT preparation, no other school provides a laboratory environment close to this.

coach.name: ⚠ No Varsity Head Coach
coach.title: UF does not field a varsity men's soccer team
coach.profile: UF does not have a varsity men's soccer program. Only a student-run club soccer team exists (gatorsclubsoccer.com). Do not contact UF Athletics for soccer recruitment — there is no scholarship pathway. Listed only because the APK degree is the #1 ranked exercise science program nationally.

rec: ⚠ NOT A VIABLE OPTION FOR OLIVIER. UF does not field a varsity men's soccer team — only club soccer exists. Olivier cannot be recruited, scholarshipped, or play NCAA D1 here. The APK degree remains academically the #1 ranked exercise science program nationally and the closest structural match to ACU BESS (16/16), so UF is retained in this guide for academic reference only. If Olivier wants this calibre of pre-PT degree with actual varsity soccer, the strongest equivalents are Indiana (15/16, B1G), USF (13/16, AAC, Florida warm climate), FIU (14/16, AAC, Miami), or PBA (14/16, D2 SSC, West Palm Beach).

name: Princeton

confRecord.note: Ivy League + Tourn double — first ever, undefeated

titles: 2025 Ivy League + Tournament double (first in history)
7 Ivy League titles under Jim Barlow

notable: Kevin O'Toole — 2022 R2 NYCFC
Barlow has coached 5 total MLS players
proPlayers.draftRank: Growing pipeline — program surging in 2024–2025

degreeTitle: No kinesiology major — Neuroscience or Molecular Biology pre-PT pathway
acuAlignNote: Partial — Princeton has no exercise science degree. Students take pre-PT through neuroscience or biology. Very different structure from ACU BESS. Strong sciences but not exercise-science specific.
aid: Need-based ONLY — no athletic scholarships (can cover 100% for qualifying families)

gpa.note: Ivy League — no athletic scholarships. Typical admitted student GPA is 3.9+. GPA 2.8 is far below.

fin.internationalNote: NO athletic scholarships. 100% need-based aid only. Princeton meets full demonstrated financial need — families below $100k AUD may receive significant grants. Average aid package covers ~75% of costs.

culture.vibe: Highly intellectual, traditions-focused, architecturally stunning. Princeton's Gothic campus is one of the most beautiful in the world. Academic culture is intense — 'Eating Clubs' replace Greek life as the social backbone. The town of Princeton is charming but quiet.
culture.thingsToDo: Princeton town restaurants and shops, New York City (1hr by train), Philadelphia (1hr), Delaware Water Gap hiking, Princeton Battle Monument, Nassau Street cultural events. NYC and Philly are weekend escapes.
culture.socialScene: Very small, intimate, elite. International students well-represented at graduate level; undergraduate international community is smaller. Honour code is sacred. Soccer team is a close-knit group. Eating club selection is a social rite of passage.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Princeton town is quiet. Culture is highly academic and competitive. If Olivier gets in and excels, the alumni network is unmatched. But day-to-day atmosphere is more studious than social.
culture.lifestyleTags: Elite academic, historic, intellectually intense

facilityDetails.stadium: Roberts Stadium — 3,000 capacity, natural grass, Ivy League standards. Historic setting on Princeton's UNESCO campus. Traditional atmosphere with no commercial signage — pure athletic environment.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 natural grass training fields adjacent to Roberts Stadium. Bubble dome available for winter training (Princeton Athletic Complex). Princeton's maintenance standards mean surfaces are immaculate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Princeton Athletic Performance Center — 15,000 sq ft, rebuilt 2018. Full Olympic lifting, Keiser systems, functional training area. No professional athletes share these facilities — completely dedicated to Princeton varsity sports. Sports science and S&C coaching available to all varsity athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Princeton University facilities are world-class at the research level — but less integrated into athletic programmes than schools with exercise science departments. Video analysis available. No formal GPS tracking system confirmed for soccer. Princeton's research resources (cyclotron, genomics labs) are world-class but less directly applicable to sport performance.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Excellent athletic medicine — Princeton University Health Services with on-site sports physician. Princeton Medical Center provides imaging and surgical consultation. Certifed athletic trainers assigned per sport. Academic medical access is excellent; sport science integration into training is less formalised.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Princeton does not offer a kinesiology or exercise science major. Biology, neuroscience, and molecular biology departments have extraordinary lab resources — world-leading at the research level. For a pre-PT student, course electives in biology and neuroscience are strong, but the structured pre-PT pathway does not exist in the way it does at UF or Indiana.
facilityDetails.extras: Princeton Art Museum (free, world-class collection). Princeton Science Library. Nassau Street cultural district. Outdoor tennis, squash, rowing on Lake Carnegie. 600 acres of arboretum adjacent to campus. 90-minute train to NYC for pro games and cultural access.
facilityDetails.note: Princeton's facilities are beautiful and well-resourced by Ivy League standards, but the absence of a kinesiology/exercise science department means the academic lab integration Olivier needs for DPT preparation is limited. Athletic facilities are top-quality but not the science environment.

coach.name: Jim Barlow
coach.title: Head Coach (29th season 2025)
coach.profile: 29th season at Princeton 2025. Career record 237-184-72. 7 Ivy League titles. First-ever Ivy League + Tournament double 2025 (undefeated 7-0 conf, no goals conceded in conf play). 2025 Northeast Regional Staff of Year. 2024 Ivy League Tournament Champions. 3 consecutive perfect Ivy seasons. Former US U-15 HC 2004-11. Princeton alum 1990 Ivy League Player of Year. Former midfielder.

rec: Princeton is surging — 2024 AND 2025 Ivy Tournament champions, first Ivy team ever to do the double. Jim Barlow coached the 2025 team to an undefeated conference season. Zero athletic scholarships but need-based aid can cover full cost. GPA 2.8 will be a significant admission challenge. Degree is not exercise science aligned.

staff.name: Steve Totten
staff.bg: 18 years on Princeton staff. Key recruiting architect. Part of 2025 Northeast Regional Staff of Year.

staff.name: Sam Maira
staff.bg: Joined 2021. 5 seasons. South Jersey Soccer Coaches HOF. Rutgers alum. Part of 2025 Northeast Regional Staff of Year.

staff.name: Tom Moffat
staff.bg: 19 seasons with the Tigers. Part of 2025 Northeast Regional Staff of Year.

name: Yale

confRecord.note: Mid-table Ivy finish. Princeton won 2025 Ivy Tournament.

notable: Occasional USL/lower league signings
NYC/New Haven clinical sports network access
proPlayers.draftRank: Limited MLS pipeline — academic-first program

kinRank: No kinesiology — Biology/Human Biology pre-PT pathway
degreeTitle: No kinesiology major — Human Biology or Ecology & Evolutionary Biology pre-PT pathway
acuAlignNote: Partial — no exercise science degree. Pre-PT through general biology. Very different from ACU BESS structure. Yale's strength is academic prestige, not exercise science specificity.

gpa.note: Ivy League — no athletic scholarships. Typical admitted student GPA is 3.9+. GPA 2.8 is far below.

fin.internationalNote: NO athletic scholarships. 100% need-based aid only. Yale financial aid for international students is generous — families under $75k income typically pay <$0. Admission is the barrier.

culture.vibe: Intellectually vibrant with genuine warmth — Yale is less formal than Princeton. New Haven is a real city with pizza culture (literally Yale vs. Frank Pepe's is a thing), a thriving arts scene, and direct train access to NYC.
culture.thingsToDo: New Haven pizza institutions (Frank Pepe's, Sally's), Long Island Sound beaches, Yale Art Gallery, Yale Peabody Museum, New Haven Green, NYC (2hr by train), hiking on East Rock. New Haven is more liveable than people expect.
culture.socialScene: Extremely diverse intellectually. Yale residential college system creates tight sub-communities within the larger university. International students prominent. Theatre and arts culture very active.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ New Haven is more accessible than Princeton's town. Yale culture blends intensity with warmth. Train to NYC makes weekends exciting. Cold winters are the main lifestyle gap for a Sydney native.
culture.lifestyleTags: Ivy intellectual, warmer culture, New Haven

facilityDetails.stadium: Reese Stadium — 3,000 capacity, natural grass, Ivy League setting. Historic Yale athletic campus. Well-maintained, traditional, understated.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 training fields at Yale Athletics. Yale's indoor facility (Lanman Center) provides covered training during New England winters. Natural grass surfaces maintained to Ivy standards.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Yale Athletics Strength & Conditioning Center — 14,000 sq ft, fully equipped with Olympic platforms, free weights, Keiser, functional training zones. Shared with all Yale varsity sports. Dedicated performance coach for spring and fall soccer preparation.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Yale Athletics video analysis (Hudl). Limited GPS tracking infrastructure confirmed for soccer. Yale's research labs (engineering, biology, neuroscience) are world-leading but not formally connected to the athletic training environment in the way exercise science schools are.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Yale Health sports medicine with certified athletic trainers. Yale New Haven Hospital (top academic medical centre) provides specialist consultation. Dr Andrew Peterson (Yale team physician) is a sports orthopaedist. Excellent medical access; formal clinical placement for pre-PT students is less structured than dedicated health science schools.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Yale has no kinesiology or exercise science major. Biology and neuroscience departments are world-class for research. Cushing Center (Yale's neuroscience building) has some of the best brain research labs in the world. For pre-PT, Yale students must build their pathway independently from neuroscience/biology electives.
facilityDetails.extras: Yale Payne Whitney Gymnasium — one of the world's great athletic buildings. Olympic pool, squash courts, climbing walls, fitness studios. Yale Art Gallery (free, world-class). Yale Repertory Theatre. New Haven Green cultural events. Train access to NYC (2hr) for weekend exploration.
facilityDetails.note: Yale's athletic facilities are impressive by any standard. However, like Princeton, the lack of an exercise science academic infrastructure means the integrated lab-to-field development pathway Olivier needs is absent. Beautiful environment but requires independent construction of pre-PT pathway.

coach.name: Kylie Stannard
coach.title: Head Coach (since 2014)
coach.profile: 2023 Ivy Tournament Champions. New Haven is smaller than NYC but more accessible. Yale's 2025 class ranked #32 nationally by Top Drawer. Note: Kylie is a male coach — unusual name.

rec: Yale won the 2023 Ivy Tournament. New Haven is a real city — more accessible than Princeton or Ithaca. No athletic scholarships. No exercise science degree. GPA 2.8 is a major admission challenge. Best considered if Olivier's GPA improves significantly and he has extraordinary academic potential.

name: PBA

confRecord.note: SSC Regular Season Champions (8-0-2 conf). Lost SSC Tourn SF to Rollins 1-2. Ranked #2 nationally.

notable: Multiple USL2 and international pro signings annually
West Palm Beach / Fort Lauderdale area has strong USL league connections
proPlayers.draftRank: D2 level — USL2 and international pro pathway

degreeTitle: B.S. in Applied Physiology and Kinesiology — Pre-Physical Therapy Concentration
acuAlignNote: HIGHEST ACADEMIC MATCH in guide. Pre-PT is in the degree concentration title. NSCA accredited. Internships at Jupiter Medical Center, Orlando Health, Paley Advanced Orthopedic Institute built into degree. Mandatory clinical placement aligns with ACU EXSC398.

gpa.note: PBA is faith-based and accessible. Athletic + academic aid stacks well. GPA 2.8 is eligible.

fin.internationalNote: PBA Christian mission scholarship adds to athletic package. Presidential Scholarship for 3.5+ GPA. Athletic + academic stacks to near-full coverage possible.

culture.vibe: Faith-based Christian university with a tight-knit, values-driven community. PBA is small (3,500 students) but West Palm Beach is a full city outside the gates. The campus atmosphere is warm, supportive, and personally focused — coaches and professors know every student.
culture.thingsToDo: West Palm Beach waterfront, Clematis Street restaurants and nightlife, Palm Beach Island luxury experience, Palm Beach Zoo, Norton Museum of Art, Fort Lauderdale (45 min), Miami (90 min), Singer Island beaches (20 min). South Florida has everything.
culture.socialScene: Small campus means everyone knows each other — close community culture. Faith values are present but not overbearing for non-religious students. Many international athletes. West Palm Beach has a cosmopolitan, Latin-influenced energy.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ PBA's tight-knit culture provides personal development support that larger schools can't match. West Palm Beach lifestyle is warm, coastal, and diverse. Best overall package for Olivier — the city compensates for the small campus.
culture.lifestyleTags: Faith community, warm coastal city, personal

facilityDetails.stadium: PBA Soccer/Lacrosse Field — 1,500 capacity, FieldTurf surface, permanent lighting. Compact but well-maintained facility on the West Palm Beach campus. SSC-standard infrastructure.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 training fields at PBA athletic complex. Additional turf field for sport-specific sessions. Year-round South Florida climate — unlimited outdoor training.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: PBA Health and Human Performance Center — rebuilt 2022, NSCA-accredited facility. Modern strength equipment (free weights, Olympic platforms, Keiser, cable systems), recovery suite (normatec, contrast baths), sports nutrition station. NSCA accreditation is a significant credential — means the facility meets national sports science standards.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) for all sessions. Video analysis (Hudl). PBA's Health and Human Performance Lab has VO2 max testing, body composition (DEXA scan), force plate assessment, and movement screening built into the athletic program — unusually sophisticated for a D2 school.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: PBA Athletics certified athletic trainer. Jupiter Medical Center formal partnership — one of the top hospital systems in Palm Beach County. Pre-PT students can access Jupiter Medical for clinical observation hours as part of their APK degree program.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: PBA Applied Exercise Science and Health department has dedicated labs: Human Performance Lab (metabolic testing, body composition), Biomechanics Lab, Sports Nutrition Lab. The Pre-Physical Therapy Concentration in the APK degree integrates directly with lab access and Jupiter Medical clinical placements.
facilityDetails.extras: PBA's faith-based campus creates a genuinely supportive community — small (3,500 students) means everyone knows the soccer team. West Palm Beach waterfront (Clematis Street) 10 minutes from campus. Singer Island beach 20 minutes. Palm Beach luxury island experience 25 minutes. Fort Lauderdale 45 minutes, Miami 90 minutes.
facilityDetails.note: PBA's NSCA-accredited facility is unusually sophisticated for a D2 school of this size. The integrated Pre-PT lab access and Jupiter Medical clinical partnership directly embedded in the degree curriculum is the standout feature — you're building clinical hours while playing competitive soccer.

coach.name: Lucas Martorana
coach.title: Head Coach (4th season as HC)
coach.profile: Named HC Nov 2022 after interim role. HC record 33-22-9 (.586). 2025 SSC Coaching Staff of Year (first as HC). PBA #2 nationally 2025 (13-3-2 overall, 8-0-2 conf). Degree in Mathematics, Masters in school counseling. Former USL/PDL/MISL semi-pro player. Was assistant/associate HC 2017-22 (61-9-6 as assistant under McMahon).

facilities: Health and Human Performance Lab (state of the art)
rec: PBA is the single best academic match for Olivier's goals in the entire guide. "Pre-Physical Therapy" is literally in the degree concentration title. Ranked #2 nationally in D2 in 2025. West Palm Beach is warm, coastal, and cosmopolitan — close to Sydney lifestyle. Mandatory clinical internship at Jupiter Medical Centre and Orlando Health.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr1 corrected: 2 juniors still present limits immediate ceiling. Steady improvement as Olivier proves himself and juniors clear.

staff.name: Jake Strachan
staff.bg: Full-time assistant. Former PBA player — earned all-SSC honors 2024.

name: Lynn

confRecord.note: SSC 2nd seed (7-2-1). Defending national champions. Dominik N-gatie SSC Offensive PoY.

notable: 3–4 USL/international signings per year
Strong Australian and UK player placement track record
proPlayers.draftRank: Best D2 pipeline for Australian players specifically

acuAlignNote: Strong — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription covered. Small campus means personal advising for DPT applications. South Florida clinical network good.

gpa.note: Lynn has no restrictive GPA entry requirement. Strong athletic profile is the primary factor.

fin.internationalNote: Lynn actively recruits international students. Merit aid available. Coach Grant has placed Australians with strong overall packages. Net cost after good offer: ~$15-20k.

culture.vibe: International, sophisticated, and intentionally global. Lynn University's student body is ~45% international — it is one of the most globally diverse universities in the USA. Boca Raton is an affluent, polished South Florida city 20 minutes from the beach.
culture.thingsToDo: Boca Raton's Mizner Park restaurants and shops, Red Reef Park beach, Delray Beach Atlantic Avenue, Fort Lauderdale Las Olas (20 min), Palm Beach (30 min), Miami (1hr), Everglades day trips. Boca has an upscale coastal lifestyle.
culture.socialScene: The most international campus culture in the guide. Cultures from 100+ countries. Australian students blend in effortlessly. Soccer team has had many international players — you won't feel isolated. Relaxed and welcoming.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★★ Lynn is arguably the best cultural fit for Olivier specifically. 45% international, warm Florida sun, 20 minutes from the beach, Boca Raton's Mediterranean-lite atmosphere. Closest day-to-day feel to Sydney expat life.
culture.lifestyleTags: Global, beach, cosmopolitan

facilityDetails.stadium: Lynn Soccer Complex — 2,000 capacity, FieldTurf, permanent lighting. Updated 2020. Located on Boca Raton campus. SSC D2 standard.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated training fields at Lynn Athletic Complex. Year-round South Florida climate allows unlimited outdoor training. GPS tracking zones on practice fields.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Lynn Athletics Performance Center — 10,000 sq ft, full free weight section, Olympic platforms, cable systems, functional training zone. Recovery station with normatec and ice baths. Shared with all Lynn varsity sports but soccer is the flagship athletic program with priority scheduling.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports GPS Elite) for all training sessions. Video analysis via Hudl with individual player dashboards. Lynn Kinesiology department has exercise physiology and biomechanics teaching labs.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Lynn Athletics certified athletic trainer. Boca Raton Regional Hospital sports medicine partnership. Lynn's close relationship with international players (including Australians) means the athletic trainer is experienced with players from different health systems.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Lynn University College of Arts and Sciences: exercise science labs for physiology and biomechanics teaching. Small class sizes (avg 17 students) mean laboratory access is less competitive than at larger universities — guaranteed bench time for every student.
facilityDetails.extras: Boca Raton's Mizner Park (10 min) — outdoor restaurants, cinema, cultural events. Red Reef Park beach (10 min drive). FAU Stadium nearby for large-scale events. Fort Lauderdale 20 min. Palm Beach 30 min. Miami 1 hour.
facilityDetails.note: Lynn's facilities are functional D2 standard — good, not elite. The standout is the small class size advantage in academic labs (guaranteed bench time) and Coach Grant's specific experience integrating international athletes, including Australians, into the program.

coach.name: John Rootes
coach.title: Head Coach (17th season — since 2008)
coach.profile: 17th season at Lynn 2025. 194-54-31 (.751) at Lynn. 3 NCAA D2 National Championships (2012, 2014, 2024). 2024 National Staff of Year. 10 NCAA appearances. 4 final game appearances. 26 All-Americans. Career total 498-150-55 across 4 schools including SNHU (169-30-11, 1989 national champion) and Clayton State (69-38-9). 2012-14 teams had highest team GPA in all NCAA/NAIA institutions.

rec: Top pick for Australian players specifically. Warm Boca Raton sunshine, large international community, personal coaching on small campus. Coach Grant has Australian connections. Exercise Science to pre-PT pathway available. Best Sydney lifestyle match at D2 level.

staff.name: Anthony Desperito
staff.bg: Former Lynn player who scored game-winning goal in 2012 national championship final under Rootes. Won 2012, 2014, 2024 titles — 2 as player, 2 as coach. First person in Lynn history to win national title as both player and coach.

staff.name: Mariana Mazzocca
staff.bg: Athletic trainer for mens soccer since 2023.

notable: 3–4 USL/international signings annually
Inter Miami CF / Fort Lauderdale CF connections
Multiple European lower league placements
proPlayers.draftRank: Best D2 all-time program for total pro pipeline — 4 national titles

degreeTitle: B.S. in Exercise Science / Human Performance
acuAlignNote: Strong — includes dedicated HPS 450 Pre-Physical Therapy Seminar as formal degree requirement (rare at D2 level). Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription, resistance training all covered.
prePT: Excellent — explicit HPS 450 Pre-PT Seminar in degree

gpa.note: Barry is accessible. No minimum GPA barrier for entry. GPA 2.8 qualifies for academic aid stacking.

fin.internationalNote: Barry Miami location — high academic aid stack possible. HPS Pre-PT degree has academic scholarship tracks. Total package can be very competitive.

culture.vibe: Urban Miami Shores campus with a Latin American-influenced energy. Barry is small but Miami permeates everything — the culture, the food, the music, the people. Small campus means tight personal community while the city provides endless stimulation.
culture.thingsToDo: Miami Beach (20 min), Wynwood Murals, Brickell city centre, Coconut Grove, Miami Zoo, Everglades, South Beach nightlife, Miami Heat basketball. Being in Miami means unlimited world-class entertainment.
culture.socialScene: Diverse, Latin-influenced, globally minded. Many international students — especially Latin American and Caribbean. Soccer culture very strong. Small campus means faculty and coaches are personally invested in every student.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★★ Miami is Sydney's American cousin — ocean, warmth, multiculturalism, and ambition. Barry's small campus provides personal support while Miami provides everything else. Strong cultural match for Olivier.
culture.lifestyleTags: Miami multicultural, warm, tight community

facilityDetails.stadium: Barry University Soccer Stadium — 2,000 capacity, natural grass, lights. Miami Shores location. Historic D2 program with multiple national championships.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 training fields at Barry Athletic Complex. Year-round Miami climate. Natural grass surfaces maintained well. Miami sports training culture means players have access to professional facility visits.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Barry Athletics Performance Center — 9,000 sq ft, Olympic lifting, free weights, cable systems. Sports dietitian on staff for health sciences students. Recovery equipment including normatec and contrast baths.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) for sessions. Video analysis (Hudl). Barry's School of Human Performance has dedicated labs including: HPS 310 Biomechanics Lab, HPS 320 Exercise Physiology Lab, and HPS 450 Pre-PT Seminar Lab — a structured capstone course integrating clinical observation.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Barry Athletics certified athletic trainer. University of Miami Health System partnership — Jackson Memorial Hospital sports medicine access. This is the same health system used by Inter Miami CF and the Miami Heat. Pre-PT students can observe clinical sessions at UM Health facilities.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Barry School of Human Performance: anatomy lab, exercise physiology suite, biomechanics teaching lab. The HPS 450 Pre-PT Seminar course is a formal academic requirement that integrates clinical observation — genuinely unique in D2. UM Health clinical placement access as part of degree.
facilityDetails.extras: Miami Beach (20 min drive). Wynwood Arts District (25 min). Brickell financial district. Miami Heat (Kaseya Center) 15 min. Inter Miami (Chase Stadium) 30 min. Barry's small campus (8,500 students) in Miami Shores — a beautiful, quiet neighbourhood right in Miami.
facilityDetails.note: Barry's facilities are D2 standard but the HPS 450 Pre-PT Seminar course, UM Health clinical access, and the program's 4 national championships create a uniquely rich development environment. Miami as a backdrop compensates generously for the modest campus facilities.

coach.name: Petsa Ivanovic
coach.title: Head Coach (4th season — since Spring 2022)
coach.profile: 4th season at Barry 2025. 31-18-8 overall record. Won both SSC regular season and tournament 2023 — first time in program history. 2025: 6th seed in SSC tournament after 6-9-3 season. Previously top assistant at Marshall for 5 years — helped win 2020-21 NCAA D1 National Championship (College Cup). Southeast Region Staff of Year 2019 and 2020-21. Originally from Palanka, Serbia. Played at University of Charleston and Jacksonville University.

rec: Barry has 4 D2 national titles — most ever. HPS 450 Pre-PT Seminar is unique at D2 level. Miami multicultural energy matches Sydney culture. Inter Miami CF connections. School of Human Performance is a dedicated college.

staff.name: Ivan Barriga
staff.bg: Promoted to Assoc HC. On staff since 2019 as volunteer, promoted to full-time 2020. Has helped produce 13 All-SSC players.

name: Nova SE

confRecord.note: 5th seed in SSC tournament (5-3-2 conf). Consistent SSC contender.

titles: 2021 D2 Final Four — program best season

notable: Occasional USL/NISA signings
Fort Lauderdale Strikers connections
Multiple former players in lower professional leagues
proPlayers.draftRank: Limited pipeline — health sciences career placement stronger than pro soccer

degreeTitle: B.S. in Kinesiology and Exercise Science
acuAlignNote: Strong core — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, sport psych covered. NSU is primarily a health sciences university; degree has strong clinical health science integration. Neuroscience faculty prominent.

gpa.note: NSU is a health sciences institution — moderately selective. GPA 2.8 is fine.

fin.internationalNote: NSU health sciences university has strong academic merit programs. Athletic + NSU Presidential Scholarship combination possible.

culture.vibe: Large health sciences university with a professional, career-focused culture. NSU is bigger than Barry or PBA but Fort Lauderdale provides a great backdrop. The campus vibe is more professional-school than party school — students are here to become health professionals.
culture.thingsToDo: Fort Lauderdale Las Olas Boulevard, Fort Lauderdale Beach, Broward Center for the Performing Arts, Sawgrass Mills shopping, Miami (30 min), Palm Beach (45 min), Everglades National Park, Bahamas ferry (90 min).
culture.socialScene: Diverse, health-sciences-focused, professionally oriented. Many pre-med, pre-PT, and pre-health students. International students present. Fort Lauderdale has a strong expat community — yachting capital of the world.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Fort Lauderdale lifestyle is excellent and warm. NSU culture is more serious and career-focused than typical college — which suits someone targeting DPT. Less party culture, more academic drive.
culture.lifestyleTags: Health-focused, Fort Lauderdale, professional

facilityDetails.stadium: NSU Soccer Complex — 1,500 capacity, FieldTurf, lights. NSU is a health sciences university with more investment in clinical labs than athletic facilities. Fort Lauderdale location.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields at NSU Athletic Complex. Year-round Fort Lauderdale climate. Consistent surface maintenance.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: NSU Athletics Weight Room — 8,000 sq ft, fully equipped with free weights, machines, Olympic platforms. NSU's health sciences focus means sports science and sports medicine are better resourced than the weight room alone suggests.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: NSU's Health Professions Division provides one of the best sports science lab environments at any D2 school. GPS tracking (Statsports) for soccer training sessions. NSU's own research infrastructure (Health Professions Division) includes simulation labs, anatomy labs, and clinical training centres.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: NSU Athletics certified athletic trainer. NSU has its own College of Health Care Sciences with a Doctor of Physical Therapy program on campus — this is extraordinary. Pre-DPT students can observe NSU DPT program sessions and potentially apply internally for the graduate program.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: NSU Health Professions Division: anatomy and physiology labs, clinical simulation centre, biomechanics lab. NSU's own DPT program creates an internal articulation pathway — undergraduate exercise science → graduate DPT, potentially at the same institution. NSU also has: Cardiovascular Lab, Musculoskeletal Ultrasound Lab, and Cadaveric Anatomy Suite.
facilityDetails.extras: Fort Lauderdale beach (20 min). Broward Center for the Performing Arts. Sawgrass Mills mall. Miami 30 min. Palm Beach 45 min. Bahamas ferry from Port Everglades (90 min journey). NSU's research infrastructure provides a professional career head start for health science students.
facilityDetails.note: NSU's unique advantage is its own on-campus DPT program. The possibility of an undergraduate → DPT articulation pathway at the same university is something no other school in this guide offers. Athletic facilities are modest but the clinical science infrastructure is outstanding.

coach.name: Matt McArthur
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Program best season ever in 2021 (Final Four). Fort Lauderdale location. Strong health sciences university environment.

rec: NSU is a large health sciences university — strongest academic environment of the Florida D2 programs for a student targeting PT. Fort Lauderdale is warm, cosmopolitan. 2021 Final Four shows program quality. Same SSC conference as Lynn, Barry, PBA.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr1/Yr2: 9 remaining MFs + High recruit risk limits Yr1 ceiling to squad rotation. 2 seniors clear after Yr1 but coach recruits to fill. Yr3 Olivier proven and established.

name: Cal State LA

confRecord.note: Mid-table CCAA finish. Competitive D2 West Coast season.

notable: LAFC and LA Galaxy academy access via proximity
USL San Diego Loyal connections
proPlayers.draftRank: Limited MLS pipeline; strong LA sports science career placement

acuAlignNote: Good core coverage. School of Kinesiology and Nutritional Science is a dedicated college — strong exercise science identity. LA internship network for DPT applications is extraordinary.

gpa.note: Cal State LA is very accessible. One of the most affordable options regardless of GPA.

fin.internationalNote: Most affordable in guide. Cal State system charges lower fees. Academic aid limited for internationals but base cost is already very low.

culture.vibe: Commuter-heavy urban Los Angeles campus. Cal State LA has a strong local community feel — many students commute from greater LA. The campus itself is functional rather than beautiful. The city is the true campus for CSULA students.
culture.thingsToDo: All of LA — Dodgers, Lakers, Rams, LAFC, Hollywood, Santa Monica beach, Griffith Park, Koreatown, Echo Park, Silver Lake. Cal State LA sits in East LA with immediate freeway access to everything.
culture.socialScene: Diverse — one of the most ethnically diverse universities in the USA. Predominantly commuter culture means on-campus social life is limited. City social life compensates completely. Many working students.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ CSULA itself has less campus culture than other schools. But LA is always there. Best if Olivier is comfortable building social life in the city rather than on campus. Affordable LA access is the value.
culture.lifestyleTags: LA access, commuter culture, diverse

facilityDetails.stadium: Soccer Practice Fields — on-campus FieldTurf. No dedicated match stadium — games played at South Pasadena facilities. CCAA D2 standard.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields on Cal State LA campus. FieldTurf surface. Year-round LA climate allows outdoor training 12 months.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Cal State LA Golden Eagles Athletics Weight Room — functional, adequate for D2. Free weights, Olympic platforms, cable machines. Shared with all CSULA varsity sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (basic system) for some sessions. Video analysis via Hudl. CSULA Kinesiology & Nutritional Science Department has dedicated labs: exercise physiology lab (metabolic carts, cycle ergometers), biomechanics lab (2D motion analysis), body composition lab.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: CSULA Athletics certified athletic trainer. Cal State LA Student Health Centre sports medicine service. LA County+USC Medical Centre (one of the largest in the USA) is 5 minutes from campus for serious injuries. Good medical access by virtue of LA location.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: CSULA School of Kinesiology and Nutritional Science: anatomy and physiology labs, exercise physiology suite, motor learning lab. LAFC and LA Galaxy training facility internship connections available through kinesiology department for motivated students.
facilityDetails.extras: All of LA — Dodgers, Lakers, Rams, LAFC, LA Galaxy. East LA cultural diversity — most diverse food scene in America within 5 minutes of campus. Griffith Park (200m). Los Angeles Museum of Natural History (5 min). Downtown LA (10 min). Hollywood (20 min).
facilityDetails.note: Cal State LA has modest athletic facilities — it's a commuter university and athletics is not the primary focus. But the LAFC/Galaxy proximity, the LA sports industry network, and the $28k annual cost make this the best value D2 option in the guide.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach — verify with Athletics
coach.profile: CCAA program. LA location means access to LAFC and LA Galaxy academies.

rec: Most affordable D2 in Los Angeles. Kinesiology is a flagship department. LA internship access for PT/Chiro is extraordinary. Best for Olivier if budget is the primary constraint.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr2 improves: 3 rising seniors clear after Yr1, opening significant minutes. Small squad (8 MFs) means Olivier's impact grows quickly.

name: St. Edward's

confRecord.note: Mid-table Lone Star Conference. Austin FC pipeline active.

notable: Austin FC USL2 affiliate (Austin Bold FC) — direct pipeline
Several USL Championship signings
proPlayers.draftRank: Developing pipeline; Austin FC connection unique advantage

acuAlignNote: Good core coverage — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription all present. Austin's booming health sector and Dell Medical School proximity make clinical hour access excellent.

gpa.note: St. Edward's is accessible. Athletic + merit aid stacks well at GPA 3.0+; 2.8 still eligible for athletic only.

fin.internationalNote: St. Edward's Holy Cross mission scholarship stacks with athletic. Moderate cost + strong aid = good value. Austin lifestyle premium worth it.

culture.vibe: Small, friendly Austin campus with an artistic, creative vibe. St. Edward's sits on a hill overlooking downtown Austin — the views are stunning and you can walk to Congress Avenue. Austin's culture bleeds onto campus constantly.
culture.thingsToDo: Sixth Street live music, Austin City Limits Music Festival, SXSW, Barton Springs swimming hole, Lady Bird Lake kayaking, Rainey Street bars, South Congress boutiques, UT Longhorns games nearby, Austin FC soccer at Q2 Stadium. Austin is the most fun city in Texas.
culture.socialScene: Inclusive, creative, liberal. St. Edward's Holy Cross mission creates a welcoming community. Austin's tech and creative industries attract ambitious students. International students welcome. Warm 10 months of the year.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★★ Austin is one of America's most exciting cities and growing fastest. St. Edward's intimate campus with Austin's city on your doorstep is a compelling package. Live music capital of the world. Excellent lifestyle match.
culture.lifestyleTags: Austin creative, live music, vibrant

facilityDetails.stadium: Butler Sports Complex — FieldTurf, 1,500 capacity, permanent lighting. Located on the St. Edward's hilltop campus with views over downtown Austin. LSC D2 standard.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 training fields at Butler Complex. FieldTurf surfaces. Stunning views of Austin skyline. Year-round Texas warmth — outdoor training 11 months annually.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: St. Edward's University Athletics Performance Center — 8,000 sq ft, rebuilt 2021. Olympic lifting platforms, Keiser resistance equipment, cable systems. Recovery suite with normatec and contrast baths. Brian Young has continued sports science integration at the program.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) for all sessions. Video analysis (Hudl) with individual player dashboards. Austin Bold FC (USL2 affiliate) shares some analytics infrastructure with St. Edward's soccer program — unique D2 partnership.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: St. Edward's Athletics certified athletic trainer. Dell Medical School (UT Austin affiliated, 10 min drive) sports medicine partnership. Dell Medical is one of the newest and most innovative medical schools in the USA — sports medicine access is cutting-edge.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: SEU Department of Kinesiology: exercise physiology lab, biomechanics teaching lab. Dell Medical School proximity gives motivated pre-PT students extraordinary clinical exposure — UT Austin Kinesiology faculty occasionally guest lecture at SEU.
facilityDetails.extras: Q2 Stadium (Austin FC MLS — 10 min drive). Barton Springs Pool (5 min). Lady Bird Lake kayaking (10 min). Sixth Street live music (15 min). South Congress (10 min). Austin City Limits Music Festival and SXSW both walk/rideshare distance. Most vibrant city environment of any D2 school in the guide.
facilityDetails.note: SEU facilities are modest but the Austin FC professional pipeline, Dell Medical School access, and the Austin Bold FC USL connection give this D2 program access to a professional culture that punches above its weight. Austin itself is the facility that matters most here.

coach.name: Brian Young
coach.title: Head Coach (multi-tenure)
coach.profile: Long-tenured HC at St Edwards — 16 seasons in first stint (168-104-32, .605), program reinstated 2023 for second stint. 2024 LSC Coach of Year. 9 conference championships overall. Program revived after COVID shutdown 2020. Was assistant at Brown University before SEU — helped Brown to 4 Ivy League Championships. Lone Star Conference NCAA DII Coaches Connection representative. Players placed professionally in Sweden and England.

rec: Austin is one of USA's fastest-growing cities. Possession system suits an 8/10. Small campus = personal development. Austin FC pipeline is a real professional pathway. Dell Medical School nearby for clinical placements.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Low recruit risk means no incoming flood. Small 7-man MF unit means Olivier contributes immediately and grows into leadership.

staff.name: Corey Miller
staff.bg: Promoted to Assoc HC March 2025. On staff since 2017. USSF National B License. Former Simpson College captain. Was HC at Concordia (Austin) where he built program to ASC Tournament for first time.

name: Oklahoma City

confRecord.note: Competitive SAC season. NAIA national tournament appearance.

notable: USL2 placements annually
Australian player placement into professional environments (A-League/NPL)
NAIA scholarship opportunities
proPlayers.draftRank: NAIA level — USL/international pathway

acuAlignNote: Good core coverage for NAIA level. Pre-PT seminar built in. Good core kinesiology coverage for NAIA level. Pre-PT seminar built in.

gpa.note: OCU is an NAIA school — very flexible GPA requirements. Athletic scholarship is primary driver.

fin.internationalNote: NAIA has NO maximum scholarship cap — full athletic ride possible. OCU Christian mission aid adds further. Best scholarship value for Australian player.

culture.vibe: Faith-based city campus with a passionate performing arts culture. OCU is famous for its dance, musical theatre, and arts programs — the energy on campus is creative and performative. The soccer program brings a hard-working athletic culture to balance the arts.
culture.thingsToDo: Bricktown entertainment district (walkable), Paycom Center (Thunder NBA — one of NBA's best atmospheres), Scissortail Park, Oklahoma City National Memorial, Lake Hefner, Will Rogers Park. OKC is more interesting than its reputation suggests.
culture.socialScene: Warm Christian community culture. Smaller international community but welcoming. Oklahoma City has grown significantly — food scene, arts scene, and NBA culture have transformed it. Growing international student community.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ OKC is not Sydney. But it's a real city, the Thunder culture is extraordinary,  and the NAIA scholarship flexibility is the best in the guide. Functional rather than glamorous.
culture.lifestyleTags: Christian community, OKC, Thunder culture

facilityDetails.stadium: OCU Soccer Field — natural grass, 1,000 capacity, permanent lighting. NAIA SAC standard. Simple, functional, well-maintained.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields on OCU campus. Natural grass. NAIA training standard — functional and adequate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: OCU Freede Center Athletic Weight Room — 7,000 sq ft. Free weights, Olympic platforms, cable systems. Adequate for NAIA level. S&C programming available through the athletics department.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (basic Polar team system). Video analysis (Hudl). OCU's Kinesiology program has exercise physiology and biomechanics teaching labs. Less sophisticated than D1/D2 schools but adequate for foundational exercise science education.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: OCU Athletics certified athletic trainer. Mercy Hospital Oklahoma City sports medicine partnership. Oklahoma City Thunder use the same Mercy Hospital network — Billy Martin has connections in the Oklahoma City sports community.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: OCU Division of Kinesiology: exercise physiology lab, anatomy lab. Oklahoma City University performing arts facilities (world-renowned) accessible for all students. Oklahoma City's growing healthcare sector provides clinical observation opportunities for pre-PT students.
facilityDetails.extras: Paycom Center (OKC Thunder NBA) — one of the best arena atmospheres in the NBA — 10 minutes from campus. Scissortail Park. Bricktown entertainment district. Oklahoma City arts district (Paseo). OCU has a welcoming Christian community culture.
facilityDetails.note: OCU facilities are functional NAIA standard. The NAIA scholarship flexibility (no cap), Oklahoma City's improving city environment, and the program's strong Christian community culture make this a genuine option for the right player. Billy Martin (HC since 2020) continues the 34-year legacy of founder Brian Harvey.

coach.name: Billy Martin
coach.title: Head Coach (since 2020-21)
coach.profile: Head coach since 2020-21. OCU alumnus — played for Harvey 1989-91. Was Harvey assistant for 19 years (1996-2014). Then Rose State College HC for 6 years (74-35-2, 4 Region II regular season titles, 3x Region CoY). Guided OCU men to 14-2-4 record in 2024 with SAC Tournament title and NAIA Tournament 2nd round. Also coaches womens program. Bishop McGuinness HS head coach since 2012.

rec: NAIA program with generous scholarships. Billy Martin (HC since 2020) is an OCU alumnus who served as Brian Harvey assistant for 17 years. Strong NAIA soccer tradition. Oklahoma City warm spring climate. No specific Australian player pathway currently documented.

staff.name: Harvey Paul
staff.bg: Former OCU player 2020-22. From Bristol, England. Business degree from OCU. 2nd-team All-SAC 2021. Hired 2023 after graduating.

name: Keiser

confRecord.note: Sun Conference play. Health sciences focus maintained.

titles: Health sciences institution — academic titles prominent

notable: Strong placement into clinical and health science roles in South Florida
proPlayers.draftRank: Not a pro soccer pipeline — choose for academic/PT pathway

acuAlignNote: Good clinical integration for NAIA level — Clinical Simulation Centre provides hands-on patient experience rare at undergraduate level. Directly relevant to DPT applications.

gpa.note: Keiser health sciences university — flexible entry. Focuses on student commitment over GPA.

fin.internationalNote: NAIA full ride possible. Keiser clinical science scholarships available for health-track students. Strong package for the right student.

culture.vibe: Health-sciences-first campus culture — clinical, professional, and purposeful. Keiser attracts students who know exactly what career they want. Campus social life is moderate but Fort Lauderdale is an excellent lifestyle city outside the gates.
culture.thingsToDo: Fort Lauderdale beach, Las Olas Boulevard, Wilton Manors entertainment district, Miami (30 min), Everglades, Palm Beach (45 min), Port Everglades cruise terminal. Fort Lauderdale is excellent for outdoor and waterfront activities.
culture.socialScene: Professional and career-focused culture. Students respect each other's clinical ambitions. Less social than a typical university but the Fort Lauderdale environment makes up for it. International students present in health science programs.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Keiser is functionally excellent for PT pathway but limited campus culture. Fort Lauderdale lifestyle is great. Best if Olivier's priority is maximising clinical hours and academic preparation over social campus experience.
culture.lifestyleTags: Clinical culture, Fort Lauderdale, purposeful

facilityDetails.stadium: Keiser University Soccer Complex — FieldTurf, 1,200 capacity, lights. Fort Lauderdale location. Sun Conference NAIA standard.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 FieldTurf practice fields on Keiser campus. Year-round Fort Lauderdale climate — ideal training environment.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Keiser Athletics Performance Center — 7,000 sq ft, well-equipped for NAIA. Olympic platforms, free weights, cable systems. Sports science programming through Keiser's health sciences faculty — the depth of clinical knowledge on staff is the differentiator.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking for sessions. Video analysis (Hudl). Keiser's clinical simulation labs — normally reserved for health science clinical training — are occasionally accessible to soccer athletes for performance testing.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Keiser Athletics certified athletic trainer. Broward Health sports medicine partnership. Keiser's own health sciences faculty (including exercise physiology and anatomy professors) provide an unusually deep sports medicine advisory support structure for NAIA level.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Keiser Clinical Simulation Centre — the standout facility. Features: high-fidelity patient simulation mannequins (including respiratory and cardiac scenarios), anatomy lab (preserved cadavers — rare at NAIA level), clinical nursing suites, rehabilitation simulation rooms. Pre-PT students get hands-on patient simulation experience before entering graduate school — unavailable at most US undergraduate programs.
facilityDetails.extras: Fort Lauderdale beach (15 min). Las Olas Boulevard. Broward Center for the Performing Arts. Miami 30 min. NAIA's flexible schedule (fewer travel days than D1/D2) means more time for clinical observation and study.
facilityDetails.note: Keiser's standout differentiator is the Clinical Simulation Centre — cadaver anatomy lab and patient simulation at NAIA level is genuinely rare. For a student targeting DPT or Chiropractic, this hands-on pre-graduate experience is more valuable than a bigger weight room.

coach.name: Gavin Oldham
coach.title: Head Men's Soccer Coach
coach.profile: Head Men's Soccer Coach at Keiser University. Staff includes Assistant Coach Joe Barber, Goalkeeping Coach Leo Camacho, and Athletic Trainer Vittoria Giordano. Keiser is a health sciences university — clinical simulation labs and pre-PT academic environment are core strengths. Fort Lauderdale campus, warm year-round climate, NAIA Sun Conference with generous scholarship potential.

rec: Keiser is fundamentally a health sciences university. Fort Lauderdale warm and cosmopolitan. Clinical Simulation Centre provides patient care experience before grad school. Best if academics are the priority over professional soccer.

name: Chapman

confRecord.note: SCIAC D3 season. NCAA tournament qualifier program.

notable: Some USL2/NISA post-graduation
Primary value: GPA platform for PT/Chiro grad school
proPlayers.draftRank: D3 — academic pathway program

acuAlignNote: Good alignment — explicit Pre-PT track with KIN 405 Pre-Physical Therapy Prep as required course. Orange County clinical network (Hoag Hospital, CHOC) is very strong for DPT applications.

gpa.note: Chapman is selective for D3. No athletic scholarships. GPA 2.8 is likely below typical admitted student.

fin.internationalNote: D3 — NO athletic scholarships. Merit and need-based aid only. Chapman Provost Scholarship for 3.75+ GPA. Net cost can be reduced but full coverage unlikely without very strong financial need.

culture.vibe: Small, creative, film-industry-adjacent Orange County campus. Chapman is known for its film school (Dodge College of Film) and arts culture — creative energy is everywhere. Orange is a charming historic town and the campus is beautiful.
culture.thingsToDo: Disneyland (10 min — literally), Old Towne Orange antique district, Newport Beach and Laguna Beach (30 min), Irvine Spectrum, Angel Stadium, LA day trips (45 min), hiking in Cleveland National Forest. Orange County is extremely liveable.
culture.socialScene: Welcoming, creative, affluent. Many students are from wealthy California families. International students present. Small campus means strong personal connections. Film/arts culture dominates social identity.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Orange County has Sydney-level weather and great beach access. Chapman's creative culture is engaging. D3 means less schedule intensity — more time for beach, arts, and GPA building. Disneyland proximity is ridiculous.
culture.lifestyleTags: Creative, OC beach lifestyle, artsy

facilityDetails.stadium: Griset Field — 1,000 capacity, natural grass, floodlit. Beautiful Orange County setting within the Chapman campus. SCIAC D3 standard.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 training fields at Chapman Athletic Complex. Natural grass. OC climate allows outdoor training almost year-round.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Chapman University Sandhu Conference Center Athletic Weight Room — 9,000 sq ft, high quality for D3. Full free weight section, Olympic platforms, functional training zone. Olympic sports get excellent support at Chapman despite D3 status.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) — unusual for D3. Video analysis (Hudl). Chapman's kinesiology department has dedicated sports science resources including: force plate, body composition analysis (BodPod), and reactive strength testing.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Chapman Athletics certified athletic trainer. Hoag Hospital Newport Beach sports medicine partnership — Hoag is ranked #1 hospital in Orange County. Pre-PT students at Chapman have formal Hoag Hospital clinical observation placement through KIN 405 Pre-Physical Therapy Prep course.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Chapman Kinesiology Department: anatomy lab (cadaver access — rare at D3 level), biomechanics lab (3D motion capture, force plates — exceptional for D3), exercise physiology lab, motor behaviour lab. KIN 405 Pre-PT Prep course is a formal required course that integrates Hoag Hospital observation with academic preparation.
facilityDetails.extras: Disneyland (10 min — literally). Disneyland and Knott's Berry Farm both walkable on busy weekends. Laguna Beach (30 min). Newport Beach (25 min). Old Towne Orange antique district (5 min walk). Chapman film school creates vibrant creative campus energy.
facilityDetails.note: Chapman punches significantly above its D3 weight in academic lab quality. 3D motion capture, cadaver access, and force plates at D3 level are exceptional. The Hoag Hospital KIN 405 clinical placement is the most structured pre-PT academic program in any D3 school in this guide.

coach.name: Dustin Johnson
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: SCIAC experienced. Academic support strong. Playing time guaranteed from year 1.

rec: Chapman has an explicit Pre-PT required course. Orange County is 30 min from LA. D3 = less schedule pressure — better for building GPA toward PT grad school. No athletic scholarships. Best if long-term academic career is the priority.

name: Santa Monica

confRecord.note: CCCAA conference play. Transfer pipeline to UCLA/UCSB maintained.

titles: Outstanding transfer record to D1 programs

notable: Transfer to UCLA then MLS draft is a proven pathway
JUCO value = stepping stone to D1 MLS-producing programs
proPlayers.draftRank: JUCO — value is pathway program to D1

acuAlignNote: 2-year degree. IGETC transfer curriculum covers biology, chemistry, anatomy basics. After transfer to UCLA/UCSB, full alignment achieved. Not a terminal degree — value is the transfer pipeline.

gpa.note: Santa Monica College has open enrolment — zero GPA requirement. Best if needing to rebuild academic record.

fin.internationalNote: JUCO — minimal cost. California College Promise Grant covers tuition for eligible students. Athletic grants available. Most affordable option to build GPA for transfer.

culture.vibe: Community college culture — diverse, no-nonsense, goal-oriented. Santa Monica College is famous for transfer students who are serious about getting to UCLA or another UC. The campus is workmanlike but Santa Monica itself is extraordinary.
culture.thingsToDo: Santa Monica Pier and beach (literally adjacent to campus), Venice Beach boardwalk (2km), Third Street Promenade shopping, Abbot Kinney Boulevard, LA access via Metro, Santa Monica Mountains hiking. Campus is 100m from the Pacific Ocean.
culture.socialScene: Extremely diverse — ages, nationalities, backgrounds. No campus residential culture — students come, learn, and go to the beach. Transfer focus means peers are academically motivated. Very accepting and unpretentious.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★★ Santa Monica is arguably the most Sydney-like location in America. Ocean, surf culture, warm year-round, diverse, laid-back. For a 2-year stay while building GPA, the lifestyle is extraordinary. Campus culture is secondary to location culture.
culture.lifestyleTags: Beach lifestyle, transfer focus, Sydney-like

facilityDetails.stadium: Corsair Field — SMC natural grass facility for JUCO soccer. CCCAA standard. Simple, functional.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on the Santa Monica College campus. Year-round beach climate. 100m from the Pacific Ocean.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: SMC Athletics Fitness Center — adequate JUCO weight room. Free weights, cables, basic machines. Santa Monica's proximity to world-class commercial gyms (Equinox, Gold's Gym Venice) means motivated athletes supplement easily.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Basic JUCO level. Video analysis available. SMC's Kinesiology Department has teaching labs for physiology and anatomy — good for foundational learning.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: SMC Athletic Trainer on staff. Providence Saint John's Health Center (Santa Monica) sports medicine partnership. Excellent access for a JUCO program.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: SMC Kinesiology Department: anatomy and physiology teaching labs, computer exercise physiology suite. UCLA's transfer pathway means students who thrive at SMC often access UCLA's world-class labs from Year 3 — the JUCO → UCLA lab upgrade is significant.
facilityDetails.extras: Santa Monica Beach (100m from campus — not a typo). Venice Beach boardwalk (2km). Third Street Promenade. Santa Monica Pier. Entire LA accessible by Metro Expo Line from campus. Google, Apple, and major tech campuses in Santa Monica for career networking. The best JUCO location in America.
facilityDetails.note: SMC's own facilities are JUCO standard — functional, not impressive. The differentiator is location: 100m from one of the world's great beaches, Metro access to all of LA, and a proven pipeline to UCLA where the facilities become elite. Think of this as 2 years at the beach before upgrading to a top-10 facility.

coach.name: Lee Avery
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Experienced CCCAA coach. Strong relationship with UCLA transfer advisors. Known for placing players at 4-year D1/D2 schools.

rec: THE best JUCO option for Sydney lifestyle match. Santa Monica is the most Sydney-like location in America. Best transfer record to UCLA in California. 2 years at ~$9k to prove GPA and soccer ability, then step up.

name: Miami Dade

confRecord.note: NJCAA conference play. Transfer pathway to D1 Florida programs.

notable: Transfer to Barry (D2 4 national titles) is the primary pro pathway
proPlayers.draftRank: JUCO — pathway program

degreeTitle: A.A. in Health Sciences (2-year transfer degree)
acuAlignNote: 2-year intro. Value is the transfer pipeline into Barry, FIU, or UM. Barry has HPS 450 Pre-PT Seminar — that is the target after MDC.

gpa.note: Miami Dade has open enrolment. Zero GPA barrier. Transfer into Barry/FIU after 2 years with stronger record.

fin.internationalNote: JUCO — very low cost. Florida residency discounts may apply after 1 year. Transfer pathway to Barry or FIU is the value proposition.

culture.vibe: Massive urban community college — functional, diverse, and Miami-flavoured. MDC is one of the largest colleges in America with 6 campuses across Miami. The culture is pure Miami — Latin, energetic, and aspirational.
culture.thingsToDo: All of Miami — see FIU above. South Beach, Wynwood, Little Havana, Calle Ocho, Miami Heat, Marlins, Inter Miami CF. Miami Dade campuses are embedded in different Miami neighbourhoods each with distinct culture.
culture.socialScene: Extremely diverse — one of the most diverse institutions in the USA. Spanish widely spoken. Commuter culture means limited residential social life but Miami compensates entirely. Transfer pathway to Barry or FIU is the social upgrade.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Miami lifestyle is excellent. MDC itself has limited campus culture but Miami provides everything. Best framed as 2 years in Miami building your record, then transferring to a residential campus.
culture.lifestyleTags: Miami energy, commuter culture, transfer pathway

facilityDetails.stadium: MDC Soccer Fields — Miami Dade College North Campus athletic facilities. NJCAA standard. Miami FL location.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on MDC North Campus. Year-round Miami climate. Fully outdoor training 12 months annually.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: MDC Athletics Weight Room — adequate JUCO standard. Free weights, cable machines. Miami's proximity to professional training facilities means motivated students can access more sophisticated environments.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Basic JUCO level. Video analysis. MDC Health Sciences campus has basic kinesiology teaching labs.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: MDC Athletic Trainer. Jackson Memorial Hospital (Level 1 trauma centre, top academic medical centre in Florida) is accessible via public transit from campus. Inter Miami CF stadium is visible from the MDC Health Sciences campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: MDC Health Sciences: anatomy lab, physiology teaching labs. MDC's proximity to UM Health and Jackson Memorial provides motivated pre-health students extraordinary clinical observation access as a community member of Miami.
facilityDetails.extras: All of Miami — South Beach, Wynwood, Little Havana, Brickell. Inter Miami CF (Chase Stadium) 10 min from health sciences campus. Miami Heat (Kaseya Center) 15 min. No other JUCO in the USA is embedded in a city of this global calibre.
facilityDetails.note: MDC's own facilities are basic JUCO standard. Miami is the facility — proximity to Jackson Memorial for clinical access, Inter Miami for professional soccer exposure, and UM Health for pre-PT pathway development means motivated students can build extraordinary CVs in 2 years.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: NJCAA. Transfer connections to FIU, Barry. Miami community ties.

rec: Miami gateway to South Florida 4-year ecosystem. Transfer into Barry for kinesiology degree and pre-PT seminar. Warm city, massive international student body.

name: U of Charleston

confRecord.note: Competitive MEC season. Program rebuilding under Maher.

titles: UC Health Sciences emphasis — pre-health campus culture

notable: USL League Two pathway — regional connections
Program focus on development and health sciences integration
proPlayers.draftRank: Developing — no MLS pipeline yet

acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — UC's Exercise Science degree covers anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics, resistance training, sport psychology, and includes clinical practicum hours. Pre-PT concentration maps well to ACU BESS structure. Research Methods coverage partial.

gpa.note: UC is accessible — GPA 2.8 is eligible and competitive for athletic scholarship. Exercise Science is the flagship health program.

fin.internationalNote: UC has a growing international program. Athletic scholarship packages for D2 are strong — up to 9 equivalencies split across roster. Merit aid stacks on top. Charleston WV cost of living is very low.

culture.vibe: Small private university with a health sciences focus. UC has reinvested in athletics and academics since 2010. Campus is compact and community-focused — everyone knows each other. Charleston WV is the state capital, giving it a city feel disproportionate to its size.
culture.thingsToDo: New River Gorge National Park (1 hr — world-class climbing and rafting), Elk River hiking, Capitol Market farmers market, Charleston Town Center, WV State Museum. Outdoor adventure is the dominant lifestyle draw — Appalachian culture.
culture.socialScene: Small, tight-knit. Athletics culture is central to campus identity. Health sciences students form a close peer group — pre-PT, pre-med, nursing all share labs. International students present but community is small.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Charleston WV is a city but not a major metropolis — climate is four-season with cold winters. Strong health sciences culture matches Olivier's DPT goals. Best fit if outdoor adventure and small-campus community appeal alongside strong pre-PT academics.
culture.lifestyleTags: Health sciences, small city, outdoor adventure

facilityDetails.stadium: Wehrenberg Field at Laidley Field — off-campus facility, grass surface. D2 standard. UC is rebuilding athletic infrastructure.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice facilities on campus and at Laidley Field. West Virginia climate means indoor training access important in winter months.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UC Athletics Weight Room — fully equipped for D2 program. Free weights, machines, cable systems. Sports science integration through Exercise Science faculty.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science department integrates sport science into undergraduate curriculum — students gain practical lab experience alongside athletic development. Biomechanics lab, physiology testing equipment. Faculty-athlete collaboration model.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UC Athletics certified athletic trainer. Partnership with Charleston Area Medical Center (CAMC) — one of West Virginia's largest hospital systems — provides clinical placement opportunities for Exercise Science students doing practicum hours.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: UC Exercise Science labs: anatomy and physiology lab, biomechanics assessment, exercise testing suites. CAMC clinical partnership gives pre-PT students observation and practicum access not available at most D2 programs. Pre-PT advising programme with graduate school application support.
facilityDetails.extras: Charleston, WV — state capital. New River Gorge National Park (1 hr). Appalachian outdoor culture. Low cost of living. Small campus means individual faculty attention. Health sciences campus culture with nursing, pre-med, and pharmacy students.
facilityDetails.note: UC's key differentiator is the CAMC hospital clinical partnership — giving pre-PT students real clinical exposure while still undergraduates. Exercise Science Pre-PT Track is well-structured. Small campus means high faculty access. Climate and city are less Sydney-like than Florida/California options.

coach.name: Daniel Smee
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Head Coach at University of Charleston. Strong international recruiting network — 2025 roster features players from Argentina, Brazil, France, Spain, Greece, Poland, UK, New Zealand, Colombia, Chile, Costa Rica, Kenya, China, and Australia. Jack Allan (Sr·D, Cape Schanck VIC) is currently on the roster — Australian precedent confirmed. Coaching staff of 7 including tactical advisor and video analyst.

rec: University of Charleston WV is a separate institution from College of Charleston (SC). UC offers a genuine pre-PT track with clinical hospital access through CAMC — a rare D2 advantage. Climate is cooler and city smaller than Olivier's warm-city preference. Best for athletes who prioritise clinical PT access and small-campus community over lifestyle match.

minutesOutlook.australianNote: Jack Allan (Sr·D, Cape Schanck AUS) on current roster — direct Australian precedent. Mention in outreach to Coach Smee.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Counting pure M positions only (13) — 6 hybrid D/M players excluded as they are primarily defenders. 4 pure MFs clear before Olivier arrives. 9 remain (4 seniors + 5 juniors) plus new recruits. 54-man squad is large for D2 but MF competition more manageable than the raw 19 figure suggested. Australian precedent (Jack Allan) is a direct conversation starter with Coach Smee.

name: Iowa Western

confRecord.note: NJCAA Region XI play. Competitive JUCO schedule against regional D1 JUCOs.

titles: Transfer pipeline to D1 and D2 programs across Midwest

Iowa, Iowa State, Creighton transfer connections

acuAlignNote: Partial alignment — Iowa Western covers anatomy, physiology basics, and health sciences foundations. Exercise physiology and biomechanics partially covered. Designed as a 2-year transfer pathway — upper-division ACU units align better after transfer to 4-year program.
prePT: Foundation — transfer pathway to DPT-prep program

gpa.note: Open admission community college. GPA 2.8 is fully eligible and competitive for scholarship. Iowa Western is an excellent entry point for students building GPA before transferring to a 4-year program.

fin.internationalNote: Iowa Western has an established international student pipeline — including Australian and European players. Athletic scholarships available. Very low tuition — one of the most affordable options in the entire guide. Financial pathway: 2yr Iowa Western ($14k) → 2yr transfer D1/D2 (negotiated aid) = significant long-term savings.

culture.vibe: Community college atmosphere — diverse student body from multiple backgrounds and ages. Iowa Western has a strong athletic culture — football is king in Iowa but soccer is growing. Council Bluffs is directly across the Missouri River from Omaha, Nebraska — giving access to a genuine metro area of 900,000 people.
culture.thingsToDo: Omaha (5 min across river) — Henry Doorly Zoo (world-ranked), Old Market district, Creighton University games, College World Series (June), riverboat casinos, Missouri River trails. Council Bluffs itself has Lewis & Clark Monument, Bayliss Park, and Harrah's entertainment district.
culture.socialScene: Diverse JUCO community. International students from Australia, Europe, South America well represented. Athletics provides social anchor. Omaha metro gives far more lifestyle options than a typical rural JUCO. Close access to Creighton (Big East), Nebraska (Big Ten), and Iowa (Big Ten) athletic events.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Council Bluffs/Omaha is cold in winter — not Olivier's warm-climate preference. However the JUCO pathway logic is strong: low cost ($14k), open entry, build GPA, transfer upward. Best if Olivier needs a GPA/eligibility bridge before targeting a D1 or D2 program.
culture.lifestyleTags: JUCO pathway, Midwest city, cold winters

facilityDetails.stadium: Iowa Western Soccer Fields — on-campus grass pitches. JUCO-standard facilities. Adjacent to athletic complex shared with football and basketball programs.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice pitches on campus. Council Bluffs/Omaha climate is cold October–March — indoor training essential in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Iowa Western Athletic Weight Room — functional D1 JUCO standard. Free weights, machines, functional training equipment. Shared with other varsity sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Basic sports science support through athletics department. Academic exercise science lab access through Kinesiology programme. Transfer partners (Creighton, Iowa State) provide benchmarking for development standards.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Certified athletic trainer on staff. Regional medical partnerships in Omaha metro. UnityPoint Health and Alegent Creighton Health systems in Omaha provide clinical access.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology lab for foundational anatomy and physiology coursework. Health Sciences programme designed to articulate with 4-year Bachelor's programs. Transfer agreements with Iowa, Iowa State, Drake University, and Creighton documented.
facilityDetails.extras: Omaha metro (5 min) — major league sports (Creighton basketball Big East, UNO hockey, College World Series in June). Henry Doorly Zoo (world's best aquarium). Old Market dining and arts district. Council Bluffs riverboat entertainment.
facilityDetails.note: Iowa Western's value proposition is the transfer pathway. Low cost + open admission + strong regional transfer network into D1/D2 programs makes this the Midwest equivalent of Santa Monica College. Climate is the significant drawback versus SMC for a Sydney-raised player.

coach.name: Mike Brown
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Head Coach at Iowa Western CC. Staff includes Associate Head Coach Cesar Herrera, Assistant Coach Anthony Galeana, GK Coach Caleb Hinson, and Director of Performance Grant Johannes — a professional setup for a JUCO program. Strong international recruiting: 2025-26 roster draws from Japan (6 players), Canada (5), Spain (2), Brazil, Latvia, Germany, Chile, Sudan, and New Zealand. Previously listed coach Nathan Clevenger — corrected to Mike Brown.

rec: Iowa Western CC is the Midwest transfer pathway school — the Council Bluffs/Omaha equivalent of Santa Monica College. Extremely affordable at ~$14k/yr with open admission. Best strategic use: bridge GPA to 2.8+ eligibility floor, gain 2 years of NJCAA D1 soccer film, then transfer to target D1 or D2 program. Cold climate is a significant lifestyle mismatch for a Sydney-raised player. Primary value is financial and eligibility access, not lifestyle fit.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: JUCO 2-year program — 100% of the current 2025-26 roster finishes before Olivier enters in August 2027. All 12 current MFs (4 So + 8 Fr) are completely gone. Zero roster overlap whatsoever. Olivier's competition is entirely determined by the 2026 recruiting class that Coach Mike Brown signs now. Ask the coach: 'How many central midfielders are in your 2026 class?' — that is the only relevant question. Trajectory modelled as a strong incoming recruit competing against one unknown class.

trajectory.label: Compete for immediate starts — clean slate
